output.explanation: Most similar values use the prefix pattern "scip-inf-" (e.g., "scip-inf-1/2/3/4/6"); "scip-inx-4" deviates by one character.
Most similar value is "scip-vte-1"; the leading "sxip" breaks the common "scip-..." prefix used throughout.
Within the "ami" series, values follow numeric or numeric+suffix endings ("ami-1" to "ami-5", "ami-7a", "ami-8a"); "ami-x" breaks that pattern.
The "axi" prefix appears only in rare one-off values, while the dominant matching pattern here is "ami-2"; initial "ax" is inconsistent with the common "am" family.
Within the "ami" series, values follow numeric or numeric+suffix endings ("ami-1" to "ami-5", "ami-7a", "ami-8a"); "ami-x" breaks that pattern.
The prevalent pattern is "pn-5c" in the same series; "px-5c" changes the middle letter and appears only once.
The dominant prefixes are "ami", "hf", "pn", and "scip"; this one-off "axi-4" is closest in structure to frequent "ami-4".
The dominant prefixes are "ami", "hf", "pn", and "scip"; this one-off "axi-2" is closest in structure to frequent "ami-2".
The "hf" series consistently uses hyphens ("hf-1" to "hf-4"); "hfx1" breaks the hyphenated pattern and includes an extra "x".
The "pn" series uses hyphen separators (e.g., "pn-5c", "pn-6"); "pnx5c" omits hyphens and inserts "x".
The "hf" series uses numeric endings ("hf-1" to "hf-4"); "hf-x" has a non-numeric suffix unlike the others.
Values with this structure are "scip-inf-*"; "scxp-xnf-3" contains multiple character substitutions and does not match the established pattern.
The "ami" series uses hyphens (e.g., "ami-2"); "amix2" omits the hyphen and inserts an extra "x".
The "pn" series has numeric endings, sometimes with a letter suffix ("pn-3b", "pn-5c"); "pn-xb" uses "x" where a number is expected.
The "pn" series uses numeric endings ("pn-2", "pn-4", "pn-6", "pn-7") or numeric+suffix; "pn-x" breaks that pattern.
The "hf" pattern is "hf-<number>"; "hx-1" changes the second letter from "f" to "x".
The common form is "scip-inf-1" style with hyphens; "scipxinfx1" removes separators and inserts "x" characters.
The matching frequent value is "scip-vte-1"; "vtx" deviates from the established "vte" segment.
The "hf" series is common as "hf-1" to "hf-4"; "xf-1" changes the leading letter and appears only once.
The "hf" series uses hyphens ("hf-4"); "hfx4" omits the hyphen and inserts "x".
The "ami" series uses hyphens ("ami-1", "ami-2"); "amix1" omits the hyphen and inserts "x".
The frequent matching pattern is "ami-4"; "amx-4" changes the third character from "i" to "x".
The frequent matching pattern is "ami-3"; "amx-3" changes the third character from "i" to "x".
The prevalent series is "pn-4"; "px-4" changes the second letter and appears only rarely.
The established pattern is "scip-inf-2"; "scix-inf-2" changes the fourth character from "p" to "x".
The "hf" pattern is "hf-<number>"; "hx-2" changes the second letter from "f" to "x".
The common nearby pattern is "cac-1"; "xax-1" changes both letters of the prefix and appears only once.
The "pn" series uses hyphens (e.g., "pn-6"); "pnx6" omits the hyphen and inserts "x".
